name: Answer questions from a knowledge base with HTTP / REST API without manual work for Knowledge Sync

parameters.errorMessage: Missing required field 'title' on incoming document.

options.systemMessage: You answer document questions using only the retrieved knowledge-base context. If the context does not contain the answer, say so and set needsHuman=true.

parameters.toolDescription: Semantic search over the knowledge sync knowledge base. Always call this before answering factual document questions.

parameters.text: =✅ Answer questions from a knowledge base with HTTP / REST API without ma — run completed at {{ $now.toFormat('HH:mm') }}

parameters.content:
### Receive Inbound Webhook

Entry point: listens for POST calls on `/wf06724-document`. Use the TEST url while building, switch callers to the PRODUCTION url after publishing.

Next: Required Fields Present?

### Required Fields Present?

Two-way gate on `={{ $json.title }}`. TRUE continues the happy path, FALSE takes the alternate branch.

Ports: ①→ Answer from Knowledge Base · ②→ Abort — Malformed Input

### Abort — Malformed Input

Fails fast with a clear message; triggers the error workflow instead of processing bad data.

### Answer from Knowledge Base

The AI brain: reads the input, decides which attached tools to call (and in what order), then composes the answer.

Ports: ①→ Grounded Answer Found? · ②→ Capture Failure Context

⚠ failures exit via the error rail

### Google Gemini Chat Model

Chat model powering the AI step (model: `models/gemini-2.5-flash`). Swap providers here without rewiring.

Feeds **Answer from Knowledge Base** as `languageModel`

🔑 Google AI API key

### Knowledge Base Retriever

Exposes the vector index to the agent as a semantic-search tool.

Feeds **Answer from Knowledge Base** as `tool`

🔑 Postgres connection

### Query Embeddings

Embedding model — MUST be the same model for indexing and querying, or retrieval quality collapses.

Feeds **Knowledge Base Retriever** as `embedding`

🔑 Google AI API key

### Grounded Answer Found?

Two-way gate on `={{ !($json.output || '').includes('needsHuman') }}`. TRUE continues the happy path, FALSE takes the alternate branch.

Ports: ①→ Return Answer (HTTP 200) · ②→ Log Knowledge Gap

### Return Answer (HTTP 200)

Sends the HTTP 200 response back to the caller — keep this fast; heavy work happens after/async.

Next: Stamp Completion Time

### Log Knowledge Gap

Appends a new row to 'Document Tracker' — a human-readable audit trail that doesn't require opening n8n to inspect.

Ports: ①→ Flag Gap to KB Owners · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Flag Gap to KB Owners

Posts a message to Slack (`#knowledge`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

nodes.name: Note: Flag Gap to KB Owners

parameters.content:
### Stamp Completion Time

Stamps `completedAt` with the current time for auditing.

Next: Persist Run Metadata

### Persist Run Metadata

Saves key fields onto the execution record — searchable later in n8n's execution list.

Next: Load Previous Snapshot

### Load Previous Snapshot

Reads every row from 'Document Tracker' — the read side of the store, e.g. for a digest or a downstream sync.

Ports: ①→ Compute Drift vs Snapshot · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Compute Drift vs Snapshot

Custom JavaScript: Compare current metrics with the previous snapshot

Next: Enrich with Reference Data

### Enrich with Reference Data

GET https://enrichment.example.com/v2/lookup — retries 3× with backoff on failure.

Ports: ①→ Fold In Enrichment Fields · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 API key / bearer for the target API

### Fold In Enrichment Fields

Shapes the item: writes `enrichedAt`, `enrichmentSource`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Summarize Run Stats

### Summarize Run Stats

Shapes the item: writes `runAt`, `itemCount`, `status`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Append Audit Row (Google Sheets)

### Append Audit Row (Google Sheets)

Appends a new row to 'Document Tracker' — a human-readable audit trail that doesn't require opening n8n to inspect.

Ports: ①→ Send Summary to Slack · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Send Summary to Slack

Posts a message to Slack (`#knowledge`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Capture Failure Context

Shapes the item: writes `failedNode`, `errorMessage`, `workflow`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Escalate Failure to Slack

### Escalate Failure to Slack

Posts a message to Slack (`#knowledge`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

Next: Park in Dead-Letter Queue

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Park in Dead-Letter Queue

Appends a new row to 'Document Tracker' — a human-readable audit trail that doesn't require opening n8n to inspect.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

# Answer questions from a knowledge base with HTTP / REST API without manual work for Knowledge Sync

**Pattern:** RAG Pipeline · **Level:** Production · **Trigger:** Webhook

## What it does
Answer questions from a knowledge base with HTTP / REST API without manual work for Knowledge Sync. It fires the moment an external system calls its webhook.

## Why it exists
Manual knowledge sync is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Receive Inbound Webhook → Required Fields Present? → Answer from Knowledge Base → Grounded Answer Found? → Return Answer (HTTP 200) → Stamp Completion Time → Persist Run Metadata → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to rag pipeline without manual effort
- Anyone automating knowledge sync in ai
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Has retries and error handling built in
- Keeps your systems in sync
- Saves hours of manual work each week

## Before first run
Connect credentials: API key / bearer for the target API; Google AI API key; Google OAuth2 / service account; Postgres connection; Slack OAuth2.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger — hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click → Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

Retrieval happens *inside* the agent as a callable tool, not as a mandatory step before it, so the agent decides when a lookup is actually worth the latency instead of always paying for one. The 'Grounded Answer Found?' gate treats an honest 'needsHuman' as a valid outcome, not a failure — logging the gap so the knowledge base improves beats a confident hallucination.

## Practice exercises

Already production-grade — deepen your understanding:

1. Turn the reranker knob on and compare answer quality on a deliberately ambiguous question.
2. Swap the vector store provider — only the vector store node changes, nothing else in the workflow.
3. Lower topK from 5 to 2 and see how much retrieved context the agent actually needed.

## 🛟 Error handling & retries

External calls retry **3× with 2s backoff**. Anything still failing exits through its node's error output into the red error rail → context is captured and the on-call channel is alerted. Failures are also parked in a dead-letter queue for replay. Set an n8n **Error Workflow** in settings as the final safety net.

## 🔐 Security

Never trust inbound payloads: the webhook path is unguessable. Add header auth or IP allow-listing for defense in depth. Secrets live in n8n credentials — never in node parameters.

## 🤖 AI cluster

Purple nodes form one AI unit: the root node orchestrates, sub-nodes plug in via typed ports (model / memory / tools / parser). Swap the chat model without rewiring anything else. Costs scale with tokens — keep prompts lean and cap iterations.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your knowledge sync process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
